title: content.rs in_link closure is O(n) linear scan for each match position
description: process_content builds skip_ranges (one entry per existing <a> tag) and then calls in_link(pos) for every URL/hashtag/mention match. in_link does a linear .any() scan over all skip_ranges entries. With N existing links and M new matches the check is O(N*M). On a post with many existing anchors (e.g. a heavily linked article fed via RSS) this is quadratic in the number of links. Fix: sort skip_ranges by start, then use binary search or a single merge-style pass since replacement positions are already iterated in order.
close_reason: Fixed: in_link now uses binary search O(log n) instead of linear scan.
